articles.title: Micron Signs Long-Term Auto Chip Deals
articles.summary: Micron signed deals with Qualcomm, Harman and several major auto suppliers.

articles.title: Marvell Stock's AI Bet Is Even Bigger Than You Think
articles.summary: The chipmaker has gone quiet on its old, diversified businesses, making its high-stakes wager on AI infrastructure the only story that matters now.

articles.title: Arm’s Recent Pullback Is a Gift for Long-Term Investors at Current Levels
articles.summary: Arm shares have shed nearly a third of their value since mid-June, and investors now face a fork in the road between a bargain and a value trap. Our proprietary model points firmly in one direction.

articles.title: Why Did Micron Technology Stock Fall 8%?
articles.summary: If you checked on your Micron Technology (MU) holdings on Wednesday, you might have done a double-take. After a strong prior session, the stock returned -8.0% while the broader S&P 500 returned +0.4%. The company hadn't made a misstep. In fact, it's the exact opposite, which makes the story here so compelling.

articles.title: Taiwan Semi Plans Extra $100 Billion in U.S. Investment as It Fights Intel Challenge
articles.summary: TSMC stock was dropping after it pledged another hefty U.S. investment, despite reporting a 77% increase in quarterly earnings.

articles.title: Why Is NVDA Stock The Discount Option Among Its Peers?
articles.summary: In the high-stakes world of AI chips, one company's performance metrics seem to be telling a very different story than its stock price.

articles.title: Intel Is Down 17% Over 1 Month: This Wall Street Analyst Thinks Doubles From Here
articles.summary: Intel shed nearly 20% in a month while most of Wall Street shrugged and called the stock fairly valued. One HSBC analyst just broke from the crowd in a dramatic way, and his reasoning centers on a part of Intel's business the rest of the Street has largely ignored.

articles.title: Just How Wide Is The Range Of Outcomes For Micron Stock?
articles.summary: The options market is pricing a notably wide path for this AI darling, and if you hold the shares, you are already exposed to the full, two-sided swing.

articles.title: MU Expands AI Memory Beyond Data Centers Into Next-Gen Cars — Locks In QCOM, Hyundai Mobis, And Other Auto Suppliers
articles.summary: Micron said the agreements are designed to improve long-term supply visibility and production planning as automakers accelerate the shift toward AI-enabled vehicles.

articles.title: The $43 Billion Consolation Prize For QCOM Shareholders
articles.summary: The chip giant sent owners a torrent of cash while its stock price went nowhere fast, raising a sharp question about what all that money actually bought.
